title: ES Mock 19 — Full Syllabus High Difficulty

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area of rectangle $=\text{length}\times\text{width}$.

**Working.** $12\times 5 = 60$.

**Answer.** $60$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The highest common factor (HCF) of $24$ and $36$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** HCF is the product of common prime factors at lowest powers.

**Working.** $24=2^3\cdot 3$, $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$; HCF $=2^2\cdot 3=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the values.

**Working.**
$$10+20+30+40=100$$

**Answer.** $100$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The sum of the first $20$ natural numbers is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum $=\dfrac{n(n+1)}{2}$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{20\times 21}{2}=210$.

**Answer.** $210$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A square pyramid of unit cubes is built with a $3\times3$ bottom layer, a $2\times2$ middle layer and a single top cube (figure). Compute the total number of cubes.

figure.caption: Three-layer cube stack
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 210 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='62' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='84' y='110' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='51' y='86' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='73' y='86' width='22' height='22'/><rect x='62' y='62' width='22' height='22'/></g><text x='28' y='150' font-size='8'>layers: 3x3, 2x2, 1x1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum the cubes in each layer.

**Working.**
$$N=3^2+2^2+1^2=9+4+1=14$$

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If today is Monday, what day of the week will it be after $15$ days?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Days repeat every $7$; take the remainder.

**Working.** $15\bmod 7 = 1$, so one day after Monday is Tuesday.

**Answer.** Tuesday.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Identify the grammatically correct sentence (free of a dangling modifier).
options: Walking through the tunnel, the engineer was protected by the helmet from falling debris.
Walking through the tunnel, the helmet protected the engineer from falling debris.
The helmet, walking through the tunnel, protected the engineer.
Walking through the tunnel, falling debris was stopped by the helmet.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The introductory '-ing' phrase must modify the subject that immediately follows it.

**Working.** Only the engineer can 'walk through the tunnel'; the helmet and the debris cannot. Hence the subject after the comma must be 'the engineer'.

**Answer.** 'Walking through the tunnel, the engineer was protected by the helmet ...'.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The net shown is folded into a cube. Which face is opposite face 2?

figure.caption: A cube net: a horizontal row of faces 1, 2, 3, 4 with face 5 above face 2 and face 6 below face 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)'><rect x='20' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='140' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='30' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='110' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='36' y='95'>1</text><text x='76' y='95'>2</text><text x='116' y='95'>3</text><text x='156' y='95'>4</text><text x='76' y='55'>5</text><text x='76' y='135'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a straight row of four faces, faces two apart are opposite.

**Working.** With 2 as the front, 1 is left, 3 is right and 4 is back; front (2) is opposite back (4).

**Answer.** 4.

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste $2500\,\text{kg}$ yields $0.12\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$ gas. Total gas is _____ m³.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas = mass × yield.

**Working.**
$$2500\times0.12=300.$$

**Answer.** $300$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste of $1000\,\text{kg}$ yields landfill gas at $0.15\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$. The total gas generated is _____ m³.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas $=$ mass $\times$ specific yield.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.15=150.$$

**Answer.** $150\,\text{m}^3$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: The cyclical framework underpinning an ISO 14001 EMS is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ISO 14001 follows the PDCA continual-improvement loop.

**Working.**
PDCA.

**Answer.** Plan-Do-Check-Act

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $40\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-40/100)=900.$$

**Answer.** $900\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-60/100)=400.$$

**Answer.** $400\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A tannery's discharge requires $₹4.5$ lakh of effluent-treatment (abatement) and additionally causes $₹2.8$ lakh of measurable downstream crop damage. Under the polluter-pays principle, the amount (in $₹$ lakh) the tannery must internalise is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The polluter-pays principle internalises the full externality: the polluter bears abatement **plus** damage costs (not just one of them).

**Working.**
$$4.5+2.8=7.3\text{ lakh}.$$

**Answer.** $₹7.3$ lakh

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A hyperaccumulator crop yields $20\,\text{t/ha}$ of dry biomass containing $500\,\text{mg/kg}$ of nickel. The nickel phytoextracted per hectare in one harvest is _____ g/ha.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal extracted $=$ dry biomass $\times$ tissue concentration. With biomass $20\,\text{t/ha}=20{,}000\,\text{kg/ha}$ and $500\,\text{mg/kg}$: $(20{,}000)(500)/1000$ g.

**Working.**
$$20{,}000\times500/1000=10{,}000\,\text{g/ha}.$$

**Answer.** $10\,000\,\text{g/ha}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $10$ identical sources each $80\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log10=90.$$

**Answer.** $90\,\text{dB}$

questions.stem: For the settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (MLD) and plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

**Concept.** SOR = Q/A, with Q and A read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{6\times1000}{400}=15.$$

**Answer.** $15$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $3$ identical machines each produce $85\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log_{10}3=89.77.$$

**Answer.** $89.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Which are recognised biodiversity hotspots in/around India?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Western Ghats, Himalaya and Indo-Burma are global hotspots; the Thar is not (low endemism/richness threshold).

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Western Ghats, Himalaya, Indo-Burma

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: First-order decay, $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** t½ = ln2/k.

**Working.**
$$0.693/0.231=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: The stable, self-perpetuating community reached at the end of ecological succession is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Succession culminates in a climax community in equilibrium with the environment.

**Working.**
Climax community.

**Answer.** Climax community

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: A landfill has a usable air-space of $5,00,000\,\text{m}^3$ and receives compacted waste at $50,000\,\text{m}^3/\text{yr}$. Its design life is _____ years.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Life $=\dfrac{\text{capacity}}{\text{annual fill}}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{500000}{50000}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">6</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$6+1.5=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=6.25\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $6.25\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="114" width="50" height="36" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="110" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">150</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">500</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{150}{500}\right)\times100=70\%.$$

**Answer.** $70\%$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A treatment plant receives BOD $250\,\text{mg/L}$ and discharges $25\,\text{mg/L}$. The BOD removal efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 250</text><rect x="190" y="138" width="46" height="12" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="134" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 25</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">BOD (mg/L)</text></svg>
figure.caption: BOD: influent vs. effluent (mg/L).

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta=\dfrac{S_0-S_e}{S_0}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{250-25}{250}\times100=90\%.$$

**Answer.** $90\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The optimum carbon-to-nitrogen (C/N) ratio for efficient aerobic composting is approximately
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A C/N around 25–30:1 balances microbial energy (C) and protein synthesis (N).

**Working.**
≈ 25–30:1.

**Answer.** 25–30:1

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="134" width="50" height="16" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="130" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">600</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{600}\right)\times100=86.7\%.$$

**Answer.** $86.7\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 5</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{5}}=81670.$$

**Answer.** $₹81670$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=150\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.4</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times150}{50+150}=0.3.$$

**Answer.** $0.3$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Select all statutes/instruments that are central environmental legislation in India.
options: The Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
The Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The Water Act (1974), Air Act (1981) and EP Act (1986) are the core environmental statutes; the Companies Act is corporate law.

**Working.**
First three are environmental law.

**Answer.** Water, Air and EP Acts

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A municipality proposes to mine its sole confined aquifer to exhaustion within 15 years to bankroll current expansion, leaving the next generation with no water source. This plan MOST directly violates which pillar of sustainable development?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Exhausting a resource so future generations cannot meet their own needs breaches inter-generational equity — the core of the Brundtland definition.

**Working.**
Map the scenario to the violated principle.

**Answer.** Inter-generational equity

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=100\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 30 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 20 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{100}{\pi\times6\times30\times20}e^{-30^2/(2\times20^2)}=0.00287056.$$

**Answer.** $28.706\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-60/100)=600.$$

**Answer.** $600\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $4\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times200/4})\times100=99.75\%.$$

**Answer.** $99.75\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $20\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">25</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times25}{20}=0.0625.$$

**Answer.** $0.0625\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">40</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="78" width="50" height="72" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="74" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">24</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$40\times\dfrac{50}{20}+24\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=198.8.$$

**Answer.** $198.8$

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 14.5</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(6-42/8)=14544.$$

**Answer.** $14544\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $4$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 15000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>
figure.caption: Only the producer (trophic level 1) energy is marked; upper levels follow the 10% rule. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$15000\times0.1^{3}=15.$$

**Answer.** $15$

figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$50+5\times28+2\times265=720.$$

**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A particle ($\rho_s=2650\,\text{kg/m}^3$) settles in water ($\mu=10^{-3}\,\text{Pa·s}$). Reading its diameter from the figure, the Stokes velocity is _____ ×10⁻³ m/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="20" width="180" height="140" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.06)" stroke="#1e293b"/><circle cx="150" cy="66" r="12" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><line x1="138" y1="66" x2="162" y2="66" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/><text x="168" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">d = 0.05 mm</text><path d="M150 82 l0 52 m-4 -6 l4 6 4 -6" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="158" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">v_s</text><text x="150" y="174" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">ρ_s = 2650 kg/m³, water μ = 10⁻³ Pa·s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle diameter marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = g(ρ_s−ρ)d²/18μ, with d read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{9.81\times1650\times(0.00005)^2}{18\times10^{-3}}=0.00225.$$

**Answer.** $2.248\times10^{-3}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.02\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $8$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.02\times8}=1174.$$

**Answer.** $1174$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Which species are associated with photochemical (Los-Angeles type) smog?
options: Sulphur dioxide as the chief agent
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Photochemical smog is an oxidising NOₓ/VOC–sunlight system producing O₃ and PAN; SO₂ characterises reducing/London smog.

**Working.**
O₃, PAN, NOₓ.

**Answer.** Ozone, PAN and NOₓ

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=80\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 60</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times80}{60+80}=0.286.$$

**Answer.** $0.286$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=120\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 25 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120}{\pi\times6\times25\times15}e^{-50^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00006563.$$

**Answer.** $0.656\times10^{-4}$
